ocr.text: The report presents two comprehensive tables detailing psychological well-being and caregiver assessments. In Table 1, "Areas
of psychological well-being" includes six measures: Autonomy, Environmental mastery, Personal growth, Positive relations,
Purpose in life, and Self-acceptance, with respective values across four conditions: Pretreatment, Placebo, Washout, and
Guaranà, along with two-way ANOVA statistics and comparisons. Autonomy recorded values of 64.5 ± 9.0 for Pretreatment,
65.4 ± 10.4 for Placebo, 64.9 ± 9.2 for Washout, and 64.7 ± 10.9 for Guaranà, with ANOVA results of 0.267, 62.30, 0.805 and a
comparison value of 0.921.
Areas of psychological well-being  Pretreatment  Placebo  Washout  Guaranà  Two-way ANOVA (F, DFd, P)  Guaranà vs Placebo (P)
Instrument  May  Jun  Jul  Aug
Apathy AES  60  57  52  42
NPI-Q total  18  13  9  6
NPI-Q apathy subscale  9  6  2  2
NPI-Q depression subscale  3  2  2  2
Caregiver burden ZBI  38  36  30  32
Caregiver self-efficacy RSCSE  16  16  18  22
Environmental mastery had values of 60.8 ± 9.1, 61.4 ± 10.1, 62.6 ± 8.9, and 61.9 ± 10.1, with ANOVA results of 1.207, 63.40,
0.311 and a comparison value of 0.965. Personal growth showed values of 66.9 ± 6.3, 66.3 ± 8.1, 66.0 ± 7.9, and 66.3 ± 7.8,
alongside ANOVA results of 0.382, 59.33, 0.711 and a comparison value of 1.000. For Positive relations, values were 64.4 ±
7.6, 63.3 ± 8.4, 62.7 ± 8.4, and 62.5 ± 7.9, with ANOVA results of 1.538, 59.08, 0.221 and a comparison value of 0.781.
Purpose in life values were 63.6 ± 9.2, 64.4 ± 8.2, 64.7 ± 8.8, and 63.6 ± 8.8, yielding ANOVA results of 0.722, 53.84, 0.495
and a comparison value of 0.810. Lastly, Self-acceptance recorded values of 61.4 ± 13.1, 61.7 ± 11.1, 62.4 ± 10.9, and 62.8 ±
11.4, with ANOVA results of 0.928, 57.63, 0.410 and a comparison value of 0.540. Table 2 evaluates "Instrument" outcomes
from May to August, revealing Apathy AES scores of 60, 57, 52, and 42; NPI-Q total scores of 18, 13, 9, and 6; NPI-Q apathy
subscale scores of 9, 6, 2, and 2; NPI-Q depression subscale scores of 3, 2, 2, and 2; Caregiver burden ZBI scores of 38, 36, 30,
and 32; and Caregiver self-efficacy RSCSE scores of 16, 16, 18, and 22.
